description: Parity tracking for cueapi-cli against the hosted CueAPI API. Enumerates which API surfaces this CLI covers and which it doesn't, so drift becomes visible at audit time. Per the 3-layer parity discipline (PR template + this manifest + Backlog rows for each port).
audit_methodology: Walk every endpoint in private cueapi/app/routers/ and map to a CLI command in cueapi-cli/cueapi/cli.py. Per-command, walk the corresponding private schemas/*.py to verify field-level coverage in the command's --flag set. Drift goes in `endpoints_missing` or `command_drift` keyed by command.
audit_cadence: Monthly full sweep. Per-PR diffs handled via the .github/pull_request_template.md `Parity Impact` section in the private repo.

commands_covered.cueapi logout: local credentials only — no API call
commands_covered.cueapi quickstart: wraps multiple endpoints for first-time setup
commands_covered.cueapi create: POST /v1/cues (covers entire CueCreate schema as of 0.2.x)
commands_covered.cueapi update <id>: PATCH /v1/cues/{id} (covers entire CueUpdate schema as of 0.2.x)
commands_covered.cueapi workers delete <id>: DELETE /v1/workers/{id} (PR #33; not in original manifest, added bonus)
commands_covered.cueapi agents list: GET /v1/agents (PR #28; --online-only convenience flag added in PR #40)
commands_covered.cueapi agents get <ref>: GET /v1/agents/{ref} (PR #28; aliased as `cueapi agents describe <ref>` per PR #40)
commands_covered.cueapi messages send: POST /v1/messages (PR #29; --from in X-Cueapi-From-Agent header, --idempotency-key in Idempotency-Key header)
commands_covered.cueapi message-to <recipient>: POST /v1/messages with pre-flight resolve via GET /v1/agents/{name} (PR #40; agent-directory PRD Surface 3 — universal inter-agent comm primitive replacing the 6-field cue-fire incantation). Accepts agent_id (agt_*), slug-form (slug@user), or bare name (resolved by listing agents). --mode <live|bg|inbox|webhook|auto> per PR #41 sets `delivery_mode` on the message body (Surface 6); default-omit discipline — only sent when user opts in. Server response renders `effective_delivery_mode` when present.

POST /v1/worker/heartbeat.blocker: Worker registration; manifest entry retained but deliberately skipped in the CLI port wave — the cueapi-worker package wraps this endpoint with proper heartbeat-loop semantics. Direct SDK-driven registration is redundant. Documented in `cueapi workers` group docstring.

missing_flags: --transport (currently inferred from --worker; explicit flag would be cleaner — refactor scope, not a simple flag-add)

cueapi executions get.current_display: execution metadata + outcome + evidence fields + effective payload (PR #589 ported via #26)

cueapi agents list.notes: PR #40 added --online-only as a convenience for --status online. Mutex with --status (raises UsageError before any HTTP call).

cueapi message-to.notes: Pre-flight resolve is the default to fail fast on typo'd recipients. Server signals surfaced: dedup-hit (200 vs 201) renders 'Idempotency-Key dedup hit'; X-CueAPI-Priority-Downgraded: true rendered when receiver-pair limits apply. PR #41 --mode is flag-passing only — depends on hosted Surface 6 schema-lock PR for end-to-end delivery_mode behavior.

ported_pr_history: PR #589 (expose payload on GET /v1/executions) → ported via cueapi-cli #26 (merged 2026-05-04T19:11:49Z). `cueapi executions get` pretty-prints the new `payload` field when present.

PR #590 (require_payload_override + required_payload_keys) → ported via cueapi-cli #26 (merged 2026-05-04T19:11:49Z). Adds --require-payload-override / --no-require-payload-override + --required-keys to `create` and `update`.

executions-list filter parity (server already supported, CLI catch-up) → ported via cueapi-cli #27 (merged 2026-05-04T19:14:41Z). Adds --outcome-state / --result-type / --has-evidence / --triggered-by.

Messaging primitive — agents identity surface (Phase 12.1.5) → ported via cueapi-cli #28 (merged 2026-05-04T18:08:58Z). 9 commands: create / list / get / update / delete / webhook-secret get & regenerate / inbox / sent.

Messaging primitive — message lifecycle (Phase 12.1.5) → ported via cueapi-cli #29 (merged 2026-05-04T19:24:04Z). 4 commands: send / get / read / ack. --from in X-Cueapi-From-Agent header, --idempotency-key support.

Remaining create + update flags (delivery / alerts / catch-up / verification / on-success-fire / status) → ported via cueapi-cli #30 (merged 2026-05-04T19:08:08Z). Click Choice validation on --catch-up + --status.
Executions verification surface (replay / verify / verification-pending) → ported via cueapi-cli #31 (merged 2026-05-04T19:23:30Z). verify is tri-state; --reason capped at 500 chars client-side.

Workers fleet + user-level webhook-secret → ported via cueapi-cli #33 (merged 2026-05-04T19:00:11Z). New `cueapi workers` top-level group + nested `cueapi key webhook-secret`. X-Confirm-Destructive sent automatically on regenerate paths.
PR #618 (per-fire send_at scheduling) → port in flight on cueapi-cli #37 (opened 2026-05-04). Adds --send-at to `cueapi fire`.

Agent-directory PRD Surface 3 (CLI ergonomics for messaging primitive) → ported via cueapi-cli #40. Adds top-level `cueapi message-to <recipient>` (POST /v1/messages with pre-flight resolve), `cueapi agents list --online-only` convenience flag (mutex with --status), and `cueapi agents describe <ref>` verb alias for `cueapi agents get`. Replaces per-agent reply-script anti-pattern (cue-mac-app `Scripts/cma-reply.sh` etc.) with one canonical CLI command.

Agent-directory PRD Surface 6 (delivery_mode flag-passing) → ported via cueapi-cli #41. Adds `--mode <live|bg|inbox|webhook|auto>` to `cueapi message-to` with default-omit discipline (only sent when opted in). Renders `effective_delivery_mode` from the server response. Flag-passing only — depends on hosted Surface 6 schema-lock PR for end-to-end behavior.
notes: Updated 2026-05-05 to reflect post-#40/#41 surface (agent-directory PRD Surfaces 3 + 6). The CLI is now feature-complete against the manifest's `endpoints_missing` (only the deliberately-skipped POST /v1/worker/heartbeat remains, with the tracked rationale that cueapi-worker is the canonical wrapper for that endpoint). Only `--transport` flag on `cueapi create` is still flagged in `command_drift` — that's a refactor concern (decouple from --worker) rather than a simple flag addition. Schema mirrors cueapi-python's manifest. The `endpoints_missing` and `command_drift` sections are the audit checklist.
